commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=07c7538f211514ad6a0154474d8166e232a11c37)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a helper and terminal `runEmbeddedAgent` branch that emits a user-visible error payload when a timed-out turn would otherwise return no payload, plus helper coverage.

PR surface: Source +126, Tests +57. Total +183 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: tool-execution timeouts skip the prompt-timeout branch and the terminal path can return `payloads: undefined`, but I did not run a live current-main reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor follow-up for a code blocker and real after-fix behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not auto-repair around the external proof gate.

target.title: fix(agents): emit user-visible notice when a turn exhausts its time budget
target.body:
## Problem

When an agent turn exhausts its time budget (`agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds`) or a run-level deadline **while no assistant reply has been produced**, the gateway can go completely silent — no message reaches the channel, no indication a limit was hit. From a user's seat this is indistinguishable from a hung process.

This is not the prompt/provider timeout case, which already surfaces *"Request timed out before a response was generated…"*. It's the **timeout that fires outside a model prompt** — most commonly while a tool is executing on a complex turn. In that case:

- `attempt.timedOut` is `true`, but because `countActiveToolExecutions(...) > 0` the runner also sets `timedOutDuringToolExecution = true`;
- `timedOutDuringPrompt = timedOut && !timedOutDuringCompaction && !timedOutDuringToolExecution` is therefore `false`, so the existing prompt-timeout notice (`run.ts`, the `timedOutDuringPrompt` branch) is skipped;
- with no partial assistant text there's no `incompleteTurnText`, so the run falls through to the terminal "embedded run done" path, returns `payloads: undefined`, and the reply layer (`auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`) returns silently on the empty payload array.

## Fix

Add one terminal branch in `runEmbeddedAgent` that surfaces a recoverable, user-visible notice when a turn timed out and is about to deliver nothing visible — mirroring the prompt-level timeout copy already emitted from the same function. Because the branch now returns a non-empty payload, it flows through the normal reply-delivery path (gateway-agnostic: Discord, Matrix, etc.) and never reaches the silent `return undefined`.

The decision is factored into a small pure helper, `resolveTurnBudgetTimeoutNotice(...)`, which returns the notice text only when it is correct to do so and `null` otherwise:

- keyed on **`timedOut`** — distinct from a user cancel (`aborted` / `externalAbort`), so a user-initiated stop never produces the notice;
- **suppressed** when a visible payload is already being returned (`payloadCount > 0`);
- **suppressed** for intentionally-silent turns (cron / heartbeat contexts that set `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent`);
- **suppressed** when a messaging tool already delivered a reply out-of-band (no double-notify).

A separate copy variant is used for an idle (no-output) model timeout, pointing at the provider-level timeout knob in addition to the agent run ceiling.

Net change is additive: it only produces output where there is currently none.

## Real-behavior proof

Driving the **real** `runEmbeddedAgent` terminal path with a fault-injected timed-out, tool-execution, empty-reply attempt (the exact shape that triggers the silent failure):

**Before (origin/main):**
```
[PROOF] result.payloads = undefined
[PROOF] result.meta.livenessState = blocked
```
→ the turn returns no payload; the channel stays silent.

**After (this PR):**
```
[PROOF] result.payloads = [{"text":"⚠️ I hit my time budget on this request and stopped before finishing. Ask me to continue, or simplify the request. If this happens often, raise `agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds` in your config.","isError":true}]
[PROOF] result.meta.livenessState = abandoned
```
→ a recoverable notice is delivered through the normal reply path.

(The harness drives `runEmbeddedAgent` via the existing test loader with `makeAttemptResult({ timedOut: true, timedOutDuringToolExecution: true, assistantTexts: [] })`; it is intentionally **not** part of the committed diff — the patch is kept narrow.)

## Tests

- Unit tests for `resolveTurnBudgetTimeoutNotice` covering the full decision matrix (times-out-silently → notice; idle timeout → idle variant; not-timed-out / visible-payload / intentionally-silent / messaging-delivered → `null`).
- Existing `run.incomplete-turn` suite stays green (118 passed), including the prompt-timeout-races-completion case (visible payload → no notice).
- `pnpm tsgo:core` and oxlint clean.
target.author: spencer2211

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

target.title: fix(agents): emit user-visible notice when a turn exhausts its time budget
